s: Sound travels faster in water than in air.
The Sahara is the largest hot desert on Earth.
The Eiffel Tower can grow taller in summer heat.
A group of crows is called a murder.
Venus is the hottest planet in the solar system.
Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas.
The Pacific Ocean is the deepest ocean.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth.
The currency of Japan is the won.
Mount Kilimanjaro is the tallest mountain in Africa.
The chemical symbol for sodium is So.
Antarctica is the driest continent on Earth.
There are 206 bones in the adult human body.
The Amazon rainforest produces about 20% of the world's oxygen.
Gold is heavier than lead.
Octopuses have blue blood.
The Statue of Liberty was originally brown.
Penguins live at the North Pole.
Humans only use 10% of their brains.
Diamonds are made of carbon.
The capital of Australia is Sydney.
A jiffy is an actual unit of time.
Lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun.
The Wall of China is a single continuous wall.
Goldfish have a memory span of only three seconds.
Saturn is the only planet with rings.
The human nose can detect over a trillion scents.
An ostrich's eye is bigger than its brain.
The Dead Sea is a freshwater lake.
Coffee is made from roasted seeds.
The unicorn is the national animal of Scotland.
Bananas grow pointing downward.
Honey can be used as an antiseptic.
Glass is made primarily from sand.
Humans can distinguish only seven colors.
Owls can rotate their heads a full 360 degrees.
The smallest bone in the body is in the ear.
Tomatoes are native to Australia.
Photons have zero rest mass.
In special relativity, two events simultaneous in one frame are simultaneous in all frames.
A neutron star is denser than an atomic nucleus.
The strong nuclear force operates over shorter distances than gravity.
Quantum tunneling allows a particle to pass through a barrier it classically could not surmount.
The entropy of the universe is decreasing over time.
Light bends around massive objects due to gravitational lensing.
Temperature can be negative on the Kelvin scale.
In a vacuum, all objects fall at the same rate regardless of mass.
The speed of sound is faster than the speed of light.
E=mc² implies a small amount of mass releases enormous energy.
An electron's position and momentum can both be known with perfect precision simultaneously.
Angular momentum is conserved when no external torque acts on a system.
The Sun will eventually collapse into a black hole.
Dark energy is thought to be accelerating the expansion of the universe.
Bitcoin's blockchain is controlled by a central authority.
Ethereum uses Proof of Work after 'The Merge' upgrade.
A private key can be derived from a public key.
Smart contracts on Ethereum are immutable once deployed.
The Bitcoin genesis block was mined by Satoshi Nakamoto on January 3, 2009.
A 51% attack allows an attacker to steal funds from any wallet.
Proof of Stake requires validators to lock up cryptocurrency as collateral.
Layer-2 solutions process transactions off the main blockchain to improve scalability.
The Lightning Network allows instant Bitcoin transactions without on-chain fees.
Ethereum gas fees are paid in a separate token called GAS.
A Merkle tree allows efficient verification of large datasets with a single root hash.
DeFi protocols are exclusively regulated by governments.
An NFT proves ownership of a physical asset by law.
Bitcoin mining difficulty adjusts approximately every 2,016 blocks.
Zero-knowledge proofs allow you to prove you know a password without revealing it.
In the Prisoner's Dilemma, the dominant strategy for both players is to defect even though mutual cooperation would be better for both.
A Nash Equilibrium is always the outcome that maximises total welfare.
In a zero-sum game, one player's gain is always exactly another player's loss.
The Minimax strategy is optimal in all two-player games.
Backward induction requires solving a game from the last move forward.
Every finite game has at least one Nash Equilibrium (possibly in mixed strategies).
A dominant strategy is always a best response regardless of the opponent's choice.
The Tragedy of the Commons shows that shared resources are always managed efficiently.
A Schelling point is always the most economically efficient outcome.
Cheap talk is always credible because it's costless to produce.
People typically feel the pain of losing $100 more strongly than the pleasure of gaining $100.
The sunk cost fallacy is a rational reason to continue investing in a failing project.
Anchoring bias can influence judgments even when the anchor is completely irrelevant.
The Dunning-Kruger effect predicts that genuine experts always overestimate their abilities.
Confirmation bias leads people to seek information that challenges their existing beliefs.
The base rate fallacy involves ignoring prior probability when evaluating new evidence.
Switching doors in the Monty Hall problem increases your win probability from 1/3 to 2/3.
In a group of 23 people, there is roughly a 50% chance two share a birthday.
Simpson's paradox means a trend visible in aggregate data can reverse in every subgroup.
A perfectly competitive market naturally corrects for negative externalities without regulation.
Adverse selection occurs before a contract is signed, while moral hazard occurs after.
Arbitrage opportunities persist indefinitely in efficient markets.
Price elasticity of demand measures how sensitive demand is to a change in price.
An oligopoly market structure has one dominant seller who sets prices.
The winner's curse means auction winners tend to overpay relative to true value.
In a Dutch auction, the price starts low and rises until a buyer accepts.
Utilitarianism judges an action's morality by its consequences on overall well-being.
Kant's categorical imperative requires calculating consequences before acting.
Rawls' 'veil of ignorance' asks people to design society without knowing their place in it.
The Mpemba effect, where hot water freezes faster than cold water under identical conditions, has been definitively proven to always occur.
Neutron stars can rotate hundreds of times per second.
The element with the highest melting point is tungsten.
Glass is technically a supercooled liquid that flows very slowly over centuries.
Humans share approximately 50% of their DNA with bananas.
Sound travels faster in water than in air.
Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
The human body contains more bacterial cells than human cells.
Oxygen is the most abundant element in the Earth's crust.
Diamonds are made of carbon atoms arranged in a cubic crystal structure.
The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
Antibiotic resistance is primarily caused by individual humans developing immunity to antibiotics.
The Sun accounts for more than 99% of the total mass in our solar system.
Water expands when it freezes, which is why ice floats.
A photon has zero rest mass.
Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most planets in the solar system.
The appendix has no known function in the human body.
Electrons orbit the nucleus of an atom in fixed circular paths.
Mitochondria have their own DNA separate from the cell nucleus.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 300,000 kilometers per second.
Helium is the second most abundant element in the observable universe.
All mammals give birth to live young.
Blood is red because of iron in hemoglobin binding to oxygen.
The pH scale is logarithmic, meaning pH 4 is ten times more acidic than pH 5.
Plants absorb most of their mass from soil nutrients.
Uranium-235 is the isotope used in most nuclear fission reactors.
The human eye can distinguish more shades of green than any other color.
Viruses are considered living organisms by most biologists.
Black holes emit no radiation whatsoever.
The ozone layer is located in the stratosphere.
Alzheimer's disease is a normal part of aging.
In quantum mechanics, a particle's position and momentum cannot both be known with arbitrary precision simultaneously.
The chemical formula for table sugar (sucrose) is C12H22O11.
Humans have 23 pairs of chromosomes.
The placebo effect cannot produce measurable physiological changes in the body.
Absolute zero is approximately -273.15 degrees Celsius.
All noble gases are colorless under normal conditions.
The closest star to Earth (other than the Sun) is Proxima Centauri.
Coral reefs are made primarily from the skeletons of coral animals.
Thermal energy always flows from regions of lower temperature to higher temperature spontaneously.
The double helix structure of DNA was first proposed by Watson and Crick in 1953.
A calorie (lowercase) used in chemistry equals exactly 1000 dietary Calories (uppercase).
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
The human liver is capable of regenerating itself after partial removal.
All isotopes of an element have the same number of protons.
Mercury is the hottest planet in our solar system.
The mitotic spindle is made primarily of actin filaments.
Telomeres shorten with each cell division, which is associated with cellular aging.
CRISPR-Cas9 is derived from a bacterial immune system.
The weak nuclear force is actually stronger than the electromagnetic force at very short ranges.
Cooper pairs, which enable superconductivity, consist of two electrons that attract each other through phonon interactions.
P53 is a tumor suppressor protein that initiates apoptosis when DNA damage is detected.
Entropy always decreases in an isolated system, according to the second law of thermodynamics.
The Chandrasekhar limit (approximately 1.4 solar masses) is the maximum mass of a stable white dwarf.
The Krebs cycle occurs in the cytoplasm of eukaryotic cells.
Quantum entanglement can be used to transmit information faster than light.
The Pauli exclusion principle states that two identical fermions cannot occupy the same quantum state simultaneously.
Prions are misfolded proteins that can induce normal proteins to also misfold.
The Drake equation is used to calculate the number of molecules in a given chemical solution.
Chemotherapy drugs typically work by targeting cancer cells exclusively while leaving healthy cells unharmed.
The quantum Hall effect requires extremely low temperatures and strong magnetic fields to observe.
RNA interference (RNAi) silences gene expression by degrading specific messenger RNA sequences.
The cosmological constant in Einstein's field equations was originally introduced to describe a static universe.
All chemical bonds involve the sharing of electrons.
Epigenetic modifications to histones can be inherited across cell divisions.
The half-life of Carbon-14 is approximately 5,730 years.
In a Carnot engine, the efficiency depends only on the temperatures of the hot and cold reservoirs.
The endosymbiotic theory proposes that chloroplasts and mitochondria evolved from free-living prokaryotes.
Type I supernovae result from the core collapse of massive stars.
Myelin sheaths around axons increase the speed of nerve impulse conduction.
The Bernoulli principle fully explains how airplane wings generate lift.
Photosystem I evolved before Photosystem II in the history of oxygenic photosynthesis.
Dark matter interacts with ordinary matter via the strong nuclear force.
The Golgi apparatus is responsible for modifying, packaging, and shipping proteins within the cell.
Fermat's Last Theorem has a direct application in predicting crystalline molecular structures.
MRI machines use magnetic fields and radio waves, not X-rays, to produce images.
The electric eel generates electricity using modified muscle cells called electrocytes.
T cells mature in the thymus, and B cells mature in the bone marrow.
Neutrinos have zero mass, as defined by the original Standard Model formulation before 1998.
NADH carries electrons to Complex I of the electron transport chain.
The Bohr model of the atom accurately predicts the spectral lines of multi-electron atoms.
The Coriolis effect causes water to drain clockwise in the Southern Hemisphere.
Chirality in molecules can cause one enantiomer to be therapeutic while the other is toxic.
General relativity predicts that time passes more slowly in stronger gravitational fields.
RNA can catalyze chemical reactions, acting as a ribozyme.
Hydrofluoric acid is classified as a strong acid because it completely dissociates in water.
The Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium describes conditions under which allele frequencies in a population do not change.
Reactive oxygen species (ROS) serve only destructive roles in cells and have no beneficial functions.
The Hubble constant describes the rate at which the universe is expanding.
Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and the ability to edit their own RNA.
In special relativity, two observers in different inertial frames will always agree on the simultaneity of spatially separated events.
The Zeeman effect refers to the splitting of spectral lines in the presence of a magnetic field.
Tachyons, if they exist, would always travel faster than the speed of light and could never be slowed to below it.
Glycolysis requires the presence of oxygen to proceed.
The Lamb shift is a small energy difference between hydrogen energy levels predicted by quantum electrodynamics.
Apoptosis and necrosis are two distinct forms of cell death that differ in whether they trigger an inflammatory response.
The strange quark has a positive charge of +2/3.
In quantum chromodynamics, gluons carry color charge themselves, unlike photons which carry no electric charge.
The Bell inequalities, when violated in experiments, rule out all local hidden variable theories.
Murray Gell-Mann proposed the quark model, and the term 'quark' comes from James Joyce's Finnegans Wake.
The anomalous magnetic moment of the electron has been calculated using QED to more than ten significant figures and agrees with experiment.
Topoisomerases resolve DNA supercoiling by transiently cutting both strands simultaneously in all cases.
The Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limit is analogous to the Chandrasekhar limit but applies to neutron stars.
The Aharonov-Bohm effect demonstrates that charged particles are affected by electromagnetic potentials even in regions with zero field strength.
In the standard model, the W and Z bosons acquire their mass through spontaneous symmetry breaking via the Higgs mechanism.
Chirality-induced spin selectivity (CISS) means that electrons passing through chiral molecules preferentially adopt one spin orientation.
Enzymatic reactions always follow simple Michaelis-Menten kinetics regardless of the number of substrates involved.
The Penrose process allows energy to be extracted from a rotating black hole's ergosphere.
The quantum Zeno effect states that a quantum system that is continuously observed cannot undergo any change.
Long-term potentiation (LTP) in hippocampal neurons involves AMPA receptor insertion into the postsynaptic membrane.
The Mpemba effect is explained by hydrogen bond topology and has been conclusively proven at the molecular level.
Superfluids like helium-4 below the lambda point exhibit zero viscosity and can flow up the walls of containers.
Frameshift mutations always result in a premature stop codon being introduced into the reading frame.
The topology of a closed universe is such that light could in principle circle the entire universe and return to its origin.
In bacteria, the lac operon is induced in the presence of lactose and repressed by the lac repressor protein bound to allolactose.
Quantum decoherence explains why macroscopic superpositions are not observed in everyday life.
The Unruh effect predicts that an accelerating observer in a vacuum would detect a thermal bath of particles.
CRISPR base editing can convert one DNA base to another without introducing a double-strand break.
The KAM theorem guarantees that all orbits in a perturbed integrable Hamiltonian system become chaotic.
The Josephson effect involves the tunneling of Cooper pairs across a thin insulating barrier between two superconductors.
RNA polymerase requires a primer to initiate transcription, just as DNA polymerase does for replication.
The concept of chemical potential in thermodynamics is a partial molar Gibbs free energy.
The anthropic principle is a well-established predictive physical law.
Spin-orbit coupling causes the fine structure splitting observed in atomic spectral lines.
The virial theorem relates the time-averaged kinetic and potential energies in a gravitationally bound system.
Telomerase adds DNA to chromosome ends using an RNA template that it carries as part of its own structure.
In Type II supernovae, most of the released energy is carried away by neutrinos, not by the optical explosion.
The Lorentz group is the symmetry group of special relativity and includes both spatial rotations and Lorentz boosts.
Thermophilic archaea found in hydrothermal vents use the same metabolic pathways as E. coli.
Dark energy is believed to constitute approximately 68% of the total energy content of the universe.
Phosphorylation by kinases always activates enzymes; dephosphorylation always inactivates them.
The Casimir effect produces an attractive force between two uncharged conducting plates in a vacuum.
Allosteric regulation of enzymes involves ligand binding at the active site to change enzyme activity.
The concept of 'junk DNA' has been largely revised since it was found that much non-coding DNA serves regulatory functions.
In stellar nucleosynthesis, elements heavier than iron are primarily formed through nuclear fusion releasing energy.
The 21-centimeter hydrogen line is used in radio astronomy to map neutral hydrogen gas in the galaxy.
The magnetic monopole has been observed experimentally in condensed matter systems as a quasiparticle.
Glycogen synthase kinase 3 (GSK-3) is constitutively active in the absence of Wnt signaling and targets beta-catenin for degradation.
The no-cloning theorem of quantum mechanics states that an arbitrary unknown quantum state cannot be perfectly copied.
In general relativity, gravitational waves travel at the speed of sound in the medium they pass through.
The electroweak unification was achieved theoretically before any of the predicted bosons (W, Z) were experimentally confirmed.
The chemical reactivity of a transition metal complex is entirely determined by the metal's oxidation state.
The ATP synthase enzyme rotates mechanically as it synthesizes ATP, making it a true rotary molecular motor.
Hawking radiation causes black holes to evaporate faster as they become more massive.
The Onsager reciprocal relations describe symmetry in the cross-coupling coefficients of irreversible thermodynamic processes.
Spontaneous emission in quantum optics occurs at a fixed rate independent of the electromagnetic environment surrounding the emitting atom.
The protein folding problem involves predicting tertiary structure from primary amino acid sequence, and AlphaFold2 has largely solved it for single-chain proteins.
In quantum field theory, virtual particles violate energy conservation because they exist only as mathematical artifacts in Feynman diagrams.
The existence of three generations of fermions is fully explained by the Standard Model.
Bose-Einstein condensates form when bosons cool to near absolute zero and occupy the same quantum ground state.
The binding energy per nucleon peaks at iron-56, explaining why fusion releases energy below this mass and fission releases energy above it.
The quantum eraser experiment demonstrates that which-path information can be erased to restore interference patterns.
In plants, C4 photosynthesis reduces photorespiration by concentrating CO2 around RuBisCO.
The Roman Empire's western half fell before its eastern half, which survived for nearly a thousand more years as the Byzantine Empire.
Napoleon Bonaparte was shorter than the average Frenchman of his era.
The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
The United States has never had a sitting president who was also a former general of the army.
The Black Death killed a higher percentage of Europe's population than any subsequent pandemic, including the 1918 flu.
Julius Caesar was never officially declared Emperor of Rome.
The Soviet Union and the United States were allied against Germany in World War II.
Ancient Egyptians used toothpaste.
The Hundred Years' War between England and France lasted exactly 100 years.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
The Vikings reached North America before Christopher Columbus.
The Library of Alexandria was destroyed in a single catastrophic fire ordered by Julius Caesar.
The Ottoman Empire and the British Empire both existed simultaneously during World War I.
Ancient Rome had a form of central heating called a hypocaust.
The Russian Revolution of 1917 produced two revolutions in the same year, not one.
Mahatma Gandhi was nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize but never received it.
The Meiji Restoration transformed Japan from a feudal society to an industrialized nation in less than 50 years.
Christopher Columbus died believing he had reached Asia, not a new continent.
The ancient Greeks had a word for the internet.
Abraham Lincoln held a patent for a device to lift boats over shallow waters.
The Aztec Empire was conquered by Spain with an army of fewer than 1,000 Spanish soldiers.
Winston Churchill won a Nobel Prize, but not for Peace — he won it for Literature.
The French Revolution began after the storming of the Bastille, which held thousands of political prisoners at the time.
Medieval knights regularly used gunpowder weapons in battle by the 14th century.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989, ending the division of Germany overnight by official decree.
The Mongol Empire at its peak was the largest contiguous land empire in history.
The United States Constitution originally prohibited slavery outright.
Ancient Romans used urine as mouthwash and teeth whitener.
The printing press invented by Gutenberg was the first printing technology ever developed.
The Cold War ended with a formal peace treaty signed between the US and USSR.
GDP is a reliable measure of national well-being and quality of life.
Raising the minimum wage always increases unemployment, according to the consensus of modern economists.
Hyperinflation in Weimar Germany made the currency so worthless that it was cheaper to burn banknotes for heat than to buy wood.
Free trade agreements always benefit both countries equally.
A country can run a trade deficit indefinitely without economic collapse.
The economic concept of 'comparative advantage' implies a country should only export goods it produces most efficiently in absolute terms.
The Great Depression was primarily caused by the 1929 stock market crash.
Austerity measures reliably accelerate economic recovery from a recession.
The Laffer Curve implies that cutting taxes always increases government revenue.
Inflation always decreases the real value of debt, benefiting debtors.
A budget surplus is always economically desirable for a government.
In a supply-demand model, price ceilings set below equilibrium always create surpluses.
Japan's economy entered a prolonged stagnation in the 1990s despite extremely low interest rates — a phenomenon economists call a 'liquidity trap'.
The Dutch East India Company (VOC) is considered by many historians to be the first publicly traded company.
Trickle-down economics has been empirically proven to reduce income inequality over time.
Bitcoin was invented by an individual or group using the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto, whose true identity remains unknown.
In economics, a 'Giffen good' is one whose demand increases as its price rises.
The Gini coefficient measures a country's economic growth rate.
China's economy is already larger than the United States' in terms of purchasing power parity (PPP).
The 2008 financial crisis was primarily caused by the collapse of the dot-com sector.
Compound interest was described by Albert Einstein as the eighth wonder of the world.
Stagflation — simultaneous high inflation and high unemployment — was considered impossible under Keynesian theory before it occurred in the 1970s.
In economic theory, a 'monopsony' describes a market where there is only one buyer.
The Euro was adopted as physical currency by all European Union member states.
Price gouging during a disaster, while socially controversial, can increase the supply of scarce goods according to economic theory.
The United States Federal Reserve is a government agency fully controlled by the Treasury Department.
A negative interest rate policy means banks effectively charge depositors for holding their money.
The Panama Papers and Paradise Papers revealed that offshore tax avoidance is practiced exclusively by criminals.
Adam Smith, the father of modern economics, was strongly opposed to corporations and monopolies.
The concept of 'market externalities' refers to costs or benefits that fall on parties not directly involved in a transaction.
Modern fiat currencies are backed by gold reserves held by central banks.
The internet was originally developed as a military project funded by the US Department of Defense.
Moore's Law states that transistor counts on chips double approximately every two years.
GPS technology was developed by and remains under the control of the United States military.
The first commercial email was sent in 1971.
JavaScript and Java are closely related programming languages developed by the same organization.
The first computer bug was a literal insect found in a computing machine.
Blockchain technology requires proof-of-work to function as a distributed ledger.
The HTTP protocol was invented by Tim Berners-Lee, who also invented the World Wide Web.
Linux is used more widely than Windows on servers globally.
Machine learning models can be trained using entirely synthetic data without real-world examples.
Quantum computers are faster than classical computers at every computational task.
The touchscreen technology in modern smartphones was invented by Apple.
The term 'bug' in computing was first used by Grace Hopper.
5G networks use higher-frequency radio waves than 4G, which results in shorter range per tower.
The first commercially successful personal computer was the Apple I.
SQL was originally called SEQUEL and was developed at IBM.
Encryption keys used in HTTPS are generated fresh for each session using asymmetric cryptography.
The Mars Curiosity rover is powered by solar panels.
All major social media platforms are legally required to remove misinformation under US federal law.
A neural network with more layers (depth) is always more accurate than a shallower network.
IPv4 address exhaustion has already occurred — IANA gave out its last available IPv4 blocks in 2011.
The US government has never legally required backdoors in commercial encryption products.
HTML is a Turing-complete programming language.
The concept of 'open source' software predates the internet.
The first hard disk drive, released by IBM in 1956, weighed over a ton and stored about 5 megabytes.
Claude Shannon mathematically defined the concept of information and laid the foundation for all digital communications.
Artificial intelligence systems that pass the Turing Test are considered legally sentient in some jurisdictions.
The most widely deployed operating system in the world by device count is Linux (including Android).
The 'halting problem' proved that there are mathematical problems no algorithm can solve.
George Washington was unanimously elected president in both of his elections.
The United Nations Security Council has five permanent members, all of which are nuclear-armed states.
The United States has ratified the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child.
Democracy originated in the city-state of Athens, where all residents could vote.
The US Electoral College has never produced a result where the popular vote winner lost the presidency.
Karl Marx spent most of his productive adult life living in England.
The Rwandan Genocide in 1994 was widely anticipated by US intelligence agencies, who did nothing to prevent it.
China holds a permanent seat on the UN Security Council, which was given to the People's Republic of China from the beginning of the UN.
Switzerland has been continuously neutral in all international conflicts since 1815.
The two-party system in the United States is mandated by the US Constitution.
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the World Bank were both created at the 1944 Bretton Woods Conference.
North Korea has tested nuclear weapons.
The Nuremberg Trials established that 'following orders' is a valid legal defense for war crimes.
The term 'gerrymandering' is derived from the name of Elbridge Gerry, a Massachusetts governor and US Vice President.
The CIA covertly helped overthrow the democratically elected government of Iran in 1953.
The League of Nations failed in part because the United States never joined.
The European Union awards the Nobel Peace Prize to member states that join.
The European Union itself was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2012.
Apartheid in South Africa was legally dismantled after a referendum in which only white South Africans voted.
The United States has formally declared war on another nation since World War II.
The Magna Carta was originally signed by King John of England in 1215 and was intended primarily to protect barons' rights, not common people.
The first constitution in the modern sense ever written was the US Constitution.
Adolf Hitler was born in Germany.
The Nazi Party came to power in Germany through a violent coup d'état.
Mao Zedong's Great Leap Forward caused more deaths than the Holocaust.
The Korean War ended with a formal peace treaty signed between North and South Korea.
The United States dropped only two atomic bombs in history — both on Japan during World War II.
The ancient city of Troy, long considered mythological, was confirmed by archaeological excavation in the 19th century.
Nikola Tesla held more patents than Thomas Edison.
The first programmable electronic general-purpose computer was built in the United States.
Ancient Greeks knew the Earth was spherical centuries before Columbus sailed.
Isaac Newton spent more of his intellectual energy on alchemy and biblical prophecy than on physics.
The Spanish Flu of 1918 originated in Spain.
Marie Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize and the first person to win two Nobel Prizes in different sciences.
Albert Einstein failed mathematics in school.
The discovery of penicillin was entirely accidental.
Antarctica was the last continent to be discovered by humans.
The Great Fire of London in 1666 killed thousands of people.
In ancient Rome, the Senate retained formal legislative power even during the Imperial period.
The Silk Road was primarily a single, well-maintained road connecting China and Rome.
The term 'capitalism' was popularized by Karl Marx, not by its advocates.
The Soviet Union launched the first human into space before the United States.
The US Apollo moon landings were halted because the technology became too expensive to maintain.
Tulip Mania in 17th-century Holland caused single tulip bulbs to briefly sell for more than a skilled craftsman earned in a year.
The Bretton Woods system pegged world currencies to gold via the US dollar, and collapsed in 1971 when Nixon ended dollar-gold convertibility.
Nikola Tesla died penniless and largely forgotten, while his rival Edison died celebrated and wealthy.
The British Empire at its peak controlled over 25% of the world's land surface.
The word 'algorithm' derives from the name of a Persian mathematician, Al-Khwarizmi.
Social media algorithms are neutral tools that distribute content without any built-in bias toward engagement.
The Enigma cipher used by Nazi Germany was broken solely by British codebreakers at Bletchley Park.
The Affordable Care Act ('Obamacare') included a provision establishing a single-payer healthcare system in the United States.
Cryptocurrency transactions are completely anonymous and untraceable by law enforcement.
The first person convicted using DNA evidence was a murderer, but DNA also exonerated the original suspect in the same case.
The US Declaration of Independence was signed on July 4, 1776.
The Stanford Prison Experiment's published findings have been substantially discredited due to fabrication and coaching of participants.
The Great Firewall of China also prevents Chinese citizens from accessing Google Maps.
All countries that have ever developed nuclear weapons still possess them today.
The global reserve currency has always been the US dollar since modern financial systems began.
The zero-lower bound on interest rates means central banks are completely powerless in a deflationary trap.
The first woman to lead a major democracy was Indira Gandhi of India.
The US and Soviet Union came within minutes of accidental nuclear war during the Cuban Missile Crisis without Kennedy or Khrushchev's knowledge.
TCP/IP, the foundational protocol of the internet, was designed to survive nuclear attack by routing around damage.
The term 'podcast' was coined in 2004 by journalist Ben Hammersley.
The human genome contains more genes than that of the water flea Daphnia pulex.
Moore's Law is a physical law of nature derived from semiconductor physics.
The Inca Empire had no writing system yet managed a vast administrative empire through a system of knotted strings called quipu.
The first transatlantic telegraph cable connected the US and UK in 1858, but it failed after just a few weeks of operation.
The Panama Canal was originally a French project that the United States took over after France failed.
The United States purchased Alaska from the Soviet Union.
The concept of 'net neutrality' means that internet service providers must offer equal speeds to all users.
The Roman Catholic Church officially reversed its condemnation of Galileo only in 1992.
During the Cold War, the US government ran a covert mind-control research program called MK-Ultra.
The Chernobyl disaster in 1986 is considered a contributing factor to the collapse of the Soviet Union.
Open-source licenses legally prevent companies from making money off open-source software.
The first person to describe an algorithm-based computational machine was Ada Lovelace, writing about Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine.
The Manhattan Project was carried out entirely in the United States.
The world population did not exceed one billion people until after the year 1800.
The first photograph ever taken required an exposure time of several hours.
Economies with higher income inequality consistently show higher intergenerational social mobility.
The Robber Baron era in the US ended when Standard Oil was successfully broken up under antitrust law in 1911.
The US has the highest incarceration rate of any country in the world.
The Byzantine Empire knew itself as 'Byzantine' during its existence.
HTTP status code 418 ('I'm a teapot') is an officially recognized IETF RFC, not a joke that was later removed.
The Dead Sea Scrolls were discovered by a shepherd boy who accidentally threw a stone into a cave.
The United States' national debt has been fully paid off at least once in its history.
Autocomplete and modern LLM-based AI systems both fundamentally work by predicting the next most likely token.
The P vs NP problem, one of the most famous unsolved problems in computer science, has remained unsolved since it was formalized in 1971.
0.999... (repeating) is exactly equal to 1.
The sum of all natural numbers 1 + 2 + 3 + ... equals -1/12 in the context of Ramanujan summation.
There are more real numbers between 0 and 1 than there are natural numbers.
A Bitcoin transaction is irreversible once it has at least one confirmation.
The statement 'This sentence is false' is a genuine logical paradox with no truth value in classical bivalent logic.
Infinity plus one is greater than infinity when referring to cardinality of the natural numbers.
Every even integer greater than 2 can be expressed as the sum of two primes (Goldbach's Conjecture) has been proven.
In Ethereum, smart contracts can be modified after deployment by their owner by default.
The square root of 2 is irrational.
Godel's incompleteness theorems imply that no consistent formal system can prove all truths about natural numbers.
A SHA-256 hash can be reversed to find the original input using polynomial-time algorithms.
The halting problem is undecidable, meaning no algorithm can determine for all program-input pairs whether the program halts.
Pi contains every possible finite sequence of digits (it is a normal number) — this has been proven.
In proof-of-work blockchains, a 51% attack allows an attacker to steal coins directly from arbitrary wallets.
There are exactly as many even numbers as there are natural numbers.
The Monty Hall problem: switching doors gives a 2/3 probability of winning.
A valid deductive argument with a false premise can still have a true conclusion.
Ethereum's gas fees are paid in ETH, not in a separate token.
The set of all sets that do not contain themselves cannot consistently exist in naive set theory.
All continuous functions on a closed interval are differentiable.
Bitcoin's total supply is capped at 21 million coins by its protocol.
In classical logic, a false proposition implies any proposition (ex falso quodlibet).
The number e (Euler's number) is transcendental.
Proof-of-stake consensus requires validators to perform intensive computational work.
Any polygon with n sides can be constructed with compass and straightedge if n is a prime.
The continuum hypothesis was proven to be independent of the standard axioms of set theory (ZFC).
A blockchain's immutability is guaranteed by the laws of physics, not by social/economic consensus.
In modal logic, 'possibly necessarily P' implies 'necessarily P' in the S5 system.
The birthday paradox states that in a group of 23 people, there is greater than 50% probability that two share a birthday.
Zero is a positive number.
Satoshi Nakamoto's identity has been officially confirmed as Craig Wright by a court of law.
There exists a largest prime number.
A tautology in propositional logic is true under all possible truth assignments.
NFTs guarantee the owner legally owns the underlying digital artwork.
Cantor's diagonal argument proves the real numbers are uncountable.
The axiom of choice is provably true from the other ZFC axioms.
Every Ethereum address is derived from a public key using SHA-256.
Hume's guillotine states that you cannot derive an 'ought' from an 'is'.
Zeno's paradox of Achilles and the tortoise is genuinely unresolvable with modern mathematics.
A private key in Bitcoin is 256 bits long.
If P implies Q and Q is true, then P must be true (modus ponens).
The number 1 is a prime number.
In Solidity, integer overflow was a default vulnerability prior to version 0.8.0.
Fermat's Last Theorem was proven by Andrew Wiles in 1995.
Every statement is either true or false — this is the law of the excluded middle.
Decentralized exchanges (DEXs) require users to hand custody of their funds to the exchange.
In intuitionistic logic, the law of the excluded middle is not accepted as an axiom.
A Merkle tree allows efficient and secure verification of large data structures.
The sum of interior angles of any triangle in Euclidean geometry is 180 degrees.
Immanuel Kant argued that the existence of God can be proven through pure reason.
Bitcoin uses the elliptic curve secp256k1 for its cryptographic operations.
Plato's allegory of the cave is found in 'The Republic'.
Two plus two equals four in all number systems.
Smart contracts on Ethereum are Turing complete.
Russell's paradox shows that the set of all sets is a valid mathematical object.
The P vs NP problem has been resolved, with the consensus being P = NP.
Descartes' cogito ('I think, therefore I am') is an a posteriori claim.
A hash collision in SHA-256 has never been found.
Ethereum's proof-of-stake consensus was activated with 'The Merge' in September 2022.
In mathematics, a theorem and a conjecture are the same thing.
The Turing test is a sufficient condition for determining genuine machine consciousness.
Every irrational number is also a transcendental number.
Gas in Ethereum measures computational effort required to execute a specific operation.
Leibniz and Newton independently developed calculus.
In classical logic, 'not not P' is logically equivalent to 'P'.
Blockchain transactions are completely anonymous by default on Bitcoin.
The four-color theorem has been proven, but the proof requires computer assistance.
Aristotle was a student of Plato.
In a zero-knowledge proof, the prover reveals the secret information to the verifier.
Any angle can be trisected using only compass and straightedge.
Utilitarianism holds that the morally right action is the one that produces the most good for the most people.
A double-spend attack on Bitcoin requires controlling more than 50% of the network's hash rate.
The number pi is exactly equal to 22/7.
In game theory, a Nash equilibrium is always the optimal outcome for all players.
Ethereum's ERC-20 standard defines a common interface for fungible tokens.
Gettier problems show that justified true belief is not sufficient for knowledge.
The square root of -1 is undefined in the real numbers but defined as 'i' in the complex numbers.
All DeFi protocols are immune to hacks because smart contracts are immutable.
The Banach-Tarski paradox proves that a sphere can be decomposed and reassembled into two spheres of the same size.
Nietzsche's concept of the 'eternal recurrence' was intended as a literal cosmological claim.
A stablecoin backed 1:1 by USD reserves cannot depeg.
In propositional logic, 'P AND NOT P' is a contradiction.
Proof-of-work mining is environmentally neutral.
There are infinitely many twin primes — this has been proven.
Socrates wrote extensively about his philosophical views.
A public key can be derived from a private key, but a private key cannot be derived from a public key (in practice).
In mathematics, 'almost all' has a precise technical meaning related to measure theory.
The trolley problem was introduced by Philippa Foot.
Bitcoin's scripting language is Turing complete.
Every bounded sequence of real numbers has a convergent subsequence (Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem).
Crypto wallets store your cryptocurrency.
John Rawls' 'veil of ignorance' thought experiment is central to his theory of justice.
In Bayesian reasoning, prior probabilities can never be updated.
The genesis block of Bitcoin contains a hidden message referencing a newspaper headline.
A valid syllogism guarantees a true conclusion.
Fermat's little theorem states that for prime p and integer a not divisible by p, a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p).
Ethereum gas prices are fixed and set by the Ethereum Foundation.
Solipsism cannot be disproved logically.
The set of rational numbers is countable.
All tokens on the Ethereum blockchain are ETH.
The Ship of Theseus paradox is primarily a problem about identity over time.
In elliptic curve cryptography, the discrete logarithm problem is believed to be computationally hard.
A sound argument must be both valid and have all true premises.
Bitcoin transactions are free to send.
Every finite group of prime order is cyclic.
Nihilism and existentialism make the same claims about the meaning of life.
The Byzantine Generals Problem motivates the need for consensus mechanisms in distributed systems.
A convergent infinite series must have all terms approaching zero.
Layer 2 blockchain solutions settle their final state on the main chain.
Wittgenstein argued in his later work that the meaning of a word is its use in language.
A number can be both rational and irrational.
In a DAO, governance decisions are made through smart contract-enforced voting.
The Chinese Room argument by John Searle argues that syntax alone is sufficient for semantics.
The Riemann Hypothesis has been proven for all non-trivial zeros of the zeta function.
Ethereum's EIP-1559 introduced a base fee that is burned rather than given to miners.
In an undirected graph, the sum of all vertex degrees equals twice the number of edges.
Kant's categorical imperative requires you to act only according to maxims you could will to be universal laws.
ECDSA signatures used in Bitcoin are deterministic by default in all implementations.
Every NP problem can be solved in polynomial time on a classical computer.
A private blockchain is more secure than a public blockchain by definition.
In formal logic, modus tollens states: if P implies Q, and Q is false, then P is false.
The Pythagorean theorem holds in non-Euclidean geometries.
Ethereum addresses are case-sensitive in their checksummed form (EIP-55).
Hegel's dialectic consists of thesis, antithesis, and synthesis — though Hegel himself rarely used those exact terms.
A Merkle Patricia Trie is used in Ethereum to store state.
The number of primes less than n is approximately n / ln(n) by the Prime Number Theorem.
Determinism is logically incompatible with the existence of free will under all philosophical frameworks.
A seed phrase for a crypto wallet is equivalent to the wallet itself — whoever has it controls the funds.
In mathematics, a bijection between two sets implies they have the same cardinality.
Blockchain technology was first described in the Bitcoin whitepaper by Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008.
Aristotle's three laws of thought include the law of non-contradiction, the law of identity, and the law of excluded middle.
A rollup is a Layer 2 solution that executes transactions off-chain but posts transaction data on-chain.
The halving of Bitcoin's block reward occurs every 210,000 blocks.
In fuzzy logic, a statement can have a truth value of 0.7.
All oracle problems in blockchain can be solved by using multiple data sources.
Popper's falsifiability criterion states that a theory is scientific only if it can be proven true.
The mathematical constant 'e' is approximately 2.71828.
Flash loan attacks exploit the atomicity of blockchain transactions.
An ontological argument attempts to prove God's existence from the concept of God alone.
A 256-bit symmetric encryption key provides approximately 128 bits of security against quantum attacks.
Zero-knowledge proofs require the prover to trust the verifier.
In Euclidean geometry, parallel lines never meet by definition.
Solidity's 'require' statement reverts the transaction and refunds remaining gas on failure.
The halting problem's undecidability implies that no general antivirus can detect all malware.
Utilitarianism was primarily developed by Jeremy Bentham and John Stuart Mill.
Ordinal numbers and cardinal numbers always refer to the same concept in mathematics.
In proof-of-stake Ethereum, validators who act maliciously can have their ETH 'slashed'.
The Sorites paradox (heap paradox) challenges the notion of vague predicates.
Every equivalence relation partitions a set into disjoint equivalence classes.
Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) on Ethereum is the same asset as native Bitcoin.
The incompleteness theorems apply only to formal systems that include arithmetic.
Schopenhauer identified the 'will to live' as the fundamental drive underlying all reality.
Re-entrancy attacks are made impossible by the Ethereum Virtual Machine itself.
The number of subsets of a set with n elements is 2^n.
Proof-of-work and proof-of-stake blockchains have identical security assumptions.
In classical mechanics, Laplace's demon could in principle predict all future events given perfect knowledge.
A blockchain fork always results in a new permanent cryptocurrency.
The law of large numbers guarantees that sample averages converge to the expected value.
A token can be both fungible and non-fungible simultaneously.
Ockham's Razor mandates choosing the simplest explanation that is also true.
In modular arithmetic, 7 mod 3 equals 1.
Ethereum validators must stake exactly 32 ETH to participate in consensus.
The problem of induction, as posed by Hume, has been definitively solved by modern philosophy of science.
Cryptographic hash functions are designed to be one-way and collision-resistant.
If the universe is deterministic, then an agent's choices are fully determined by prior causes.
ERC-721 defines a standard for non-fungible tokens on Ethereum.
The diagonal of a unit square is exactly 1.5 units long.
An argument can be valid without being sound.
All stablecoins maintain their peg through purely algorithmic mechanisms.
Tarski's undefinability theorem states that a sufficiently expressive language cannot define its own truth predicate.
Bertrand Russell believed that mathematics could be fully reduced to logic.
Curve25519 is an elliptic curve commonly used in modern cryptography.
In non-Euclidean (spherical) geometry, the sum of angles in a triangle exceeds 180 degrees.
A mempool in blockchain refers to a pool of unconfirmed transactions waiting to be included in a block.
In philosophy, compatibilism holds that free will is incompatible with determinism.
The number of distinct binary trees with n nodes is given by the Catalan numbers.
Sending ETH to a burn address permanently removes it from circulation.
All mathematical truths are knowable a priori — this is the position of mathematical Platonism.
The blockchain trilemma states that a blockchain cannot simultaneously be scalable, secure, and decentralized.
Plato believed that mathematical objects exist independently of the physical world.
The probability of rolling two sixes in a row with a fair die is 1/36.
Every Ethereum smart contract has a unique address that is determined at deployment time.
Nietzsche's 'will to power' is best understood as a desire for political domination.
In mathematics, an infinite set can be in bijection with one of its proper subsets.
All blockchain data is encrypted and private by default.
The principle of sufficient reason, as held by Leibniz, states that everything has a reason or cause.
A re-entrancy guard in Solidity prevents a function from being called recursively before it finishes execution.
Wittgenstein's Tractatus and Philosophical Investigations represent opposite views on language.
The concept of 'trustlessness' in blockchain means users do not need to trust the protocol itself.
A geometric series with ratio |r| < 1 converges to a finite sum.
Every public blockchain transaction is pseudonymous, not truly anonymous.
Frege's distinction between 'Sinn' (sense) and 'Bedeutung' (reference) allows two expressions to share a referent but differ in meaning.
A block producer in a proof-of-work chain is selected by lottery weighted by hash power.
Russell's paradox was resolved by introducing the theory of types.
Decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) are legally recognized corporations under U.S. federal law.
The continuum hypothesis asks whether there is a set whose cardinality is strictly between the integers and the reals.
In Ethereum, the 'msg.sender' inside a smart contract always refers to the original externally-owned account.
Epictetus and Marcus Aurelius were both Stoic philosophers.
A rainbow table attack is effective against properly salted password hashes.
The law of excluded middle is equivalent to the principle of double negation elimination in classical logic.
Vitalik Buterin is one of the co-founders of Ethereum.
All transcendental numbers are irrational, but not all irrational numbers are transcendental.
In philosophy of mind, physicalism holds that mental states are identical to physical brain states.
Ethereum's SELFDESTRUCT opcode permanently prevents a contract's address from being used again.
A proof by contradiction assumes the negation of the statement to be proved and derives an impossibility.
The hard problem of consciousness, as named by David Chalmers, concerns why and how physical processes give rise to subjective experience.
In a Nash equilibrium, no player can improve their outcome by unilaterally changing their strategy.
The prisoner's dilemma demonstrates that rational individual choices always lead to collectively optimal outcomes.
Prospect theory was developed by Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky.
Loss aversion means people feel the pain of losses more acutely than the pleasure of equivalent gains.
A dominant strategy is one that yields the best payoff regardless of what other players do.
The anchoring bias causes people to rely too heavily on the first piece of information they receive.
In a zero-sum game, one player's gain is always exactly equal to another player's loss.
The availability heuristic leads people to overestimate the probability of events that are easy to recall.
Confirmation bias is the tendency to seek out information that contradicts one's existing beliefs.
The ultimatum game consistently shows that people accept any non-zero offer when playing rationally.
A Pareto improvement makes at least one person better off without making anyone worse off.
The sunk cost fallacy involves continuing a failing endeavor because of previously invested resources.
Herding behavior in financial markets can be explained by rational information cascades.
The endowment effect causes people to value objects they own more than identical objects they do not own.
A mixed strategy Nash equilibrium requires players to randomize over their available strategies.
The dunning-kruger effect describes how experts consistently underestimate their own competence.
Hyperbolic discounting describes a preference for smaller rewards now over larger rewards in the future.
In the repeated prisoner's dilemma with a known finite endpoint, cooperation always unravels via backward induction.
The hot hand fallacy is the mistaken belief that a person who has succeeded recently is more likely to succeed again.
Moral hazard occurs when one party takes on more risk because another party bears the cost of that risk.
In a public goods game, the Nash equilibrium involves all players contributing their full endowment.
The representativeness heuristic leads people to judge probability by similarity to a prototype.
Adverse selection occurs after a contract is signed, while moral hazard occurs before.
The status quo bias describes the preference for the current state of affairs over any change.
Tit-for-tat is a strategy that cooperates on the first move then mirrors the opponent's previous move.
Mental accounting refers to the tendency to treat money differently depending on its source or intended use.
The framing effect shows that people make identical choices regardless of how options are presented.
A Schelling point is a focal point people tend to choose in the absence of communication.
The bystander effect predicts that individuals are more likely to help in emergencies when others are present.
Gambler's fallacy is the belief that past random events influence future independent random events.
In evolutionary game theory, an evolutionarily stable strategy cannot be invaded by a mutant strategy.
The tragedy of the commons occurs when individual users deplete a shared resource acting in their self-interest.
Overconfidence bias leads most people to rate their driving ability as below average.
The dictator game involves one player deciding how to split a sum and the other player having veto power.
Social proof is a cognitive bias where people assume the actions of others reflect the correct behavior.
In Bayesian game theory, players have incomplete information about other players' payoffs or types.
The planning fallacy refers to the systematic underestimation of time and costs needed to complete projects.
Reciprocity in social exchange theory means people help those who have helped them.
A subgame perfect Nash equilibrium requires that strategies form a Nash equilibrium in every subgame.
The decoy effect describes how adding a third inferior option changes preferences between two existing options.
In a coordination game, both players prefer to take the same action as the other player.
The base rate neglect fallacy occurs when people overweight specific case information and underweight general statistics.
Elinor Ostrom's Nobel Prize work showed that commons are always either privatized or regulated by governments to avoid tragedy.
In the Allais paradox, people's choices between lotteries violate expected utility theory.
The halo effect occurs when a positive impression in one domain negatively influences opinions in other domains.
Backward induction in finite sequential games involves solving from the last period backward.
In a Bertrand duopoly with identical goods and no capacity constraints, the equilibrium price equals marginal cost.
Nudge theory, associated with Thaler and Sunstein, relies on coercive mandates to change behavior.
The contrast effect causes an item to appear more attractive when compared to a clearly inferior alternative.
In regret theory, people evaluate outcomes relative to what would have happened under alternative choices.
The winner's curse in auctions means the winner typically pays less than the true value of the item.
Default options leverage status quo bias to steer behavior without restricting choices.
In common value auctions, all bidders have the same private value for the item.
The folk theorem in game theory states that in infinitely repeated games, cooperative outcomes can be sustained as Nash equilibria.
Intrinsic motivation is always strengthened when external rewards are added to previously enjoyable tasks.
The cognitive dissonance theory was proposed by Leon Festinger.
In a Cournot duopoly, firms compete on price rather than quantity.
The peak-end rule states that people judge an experience based on its most intense point and its ending, not its average.
Scope insensitivity describes the finding that people's willingness to pay does not scale proportionally with the magnitude of a problem.
The Ellsberg paradox demonstrates that people prefer known probabilities to unknown probabilities, violating expected utility theory.
In social identity theory, people derive self-esteem partly from the groups they belong to.
The mere exposure effect shows that people tend to develop a preference for things they are unfamiliar with.
In signaling theory, a costly signal is credible precisely because it is expensive to fake.
The disposition effect in finance refers to the tendency to sell winning assets too early and hold losing assets too long.
A dominant strategy equilibrium is always a Nash equilibrium.
In the trust game, the receiver always returns zero in the unique subgame perfect Nash equilibrium.
According to prospect theory, the value function is concave for gains and convex for losses.
The identifiable victim effect shows that people respond more strongly to single identified individuals than to statistical lives.
The social comparison theory posits that people evaluate their opinions and abilities against objective standards when social comparisons are available.
In screening models, the uninformed party moves first to design a mechanism that elicits private information.
The Rawlsian veil of ignorance thought experiment supports maximizing the welfare of the worst-off member of society.
Cheap talk in game theory is always credible because it is costless.
Reactive devaluation is the tendency to devalue proposals made by an adversary simply because of the source.
The Condorcet paradox shows that majority preferences can be cyclical even when individual preferences are transitive.
In a second-price sealed-bid auction (Vickrey auction), bidding one's true valuation is a dominant strategy.
Status quo bias and loss aversion are entirely independent cognitive phenomena with no relationship.
The concept of k-level thinking models agents as best-responding to opponents who think k-1 levels deep.
In an information cascade, it is rational for individuals to ignore their private signals and follow the crowd.
The Nash bargaining solution always gives all surplus to the player with higher bargaining power.
Regression to the mean is a statistical phenomenon that is often mistakenly attributed to a causal intervention.
The Keynesian beauty contest metaphor describes investors trying to predict market fundamentals rather than others' expectations.
In mechanism design, the revelation principle states that any equilibrium outcome can be replicated by a direct mechanism where truthful reporting is optimal.
Overconfidence in forecasting is more pronounced for difficult tasks than for easy tasks.
The Coase theorem holds that in the absence of transaction costs, private parties will bargain to efficient outcomes regardless of the initial assignment of property rights.
Psychological reactance is the tendency to embrace restrictions to one's freedom of choice.
In quantal response equilibrium, players always choose the strategy with the highest expected payoff.
The hot stove effect describes how people overgeneralize from a single vivid negative experience.
Arrow's impossibility theorem proves that no voting system can simultaneously satisfy a set of reasonable fairness criteria when there are three or more candidates.
Altruistic punishment refers to the willingness of individuals to punish norm violators even at a cost to themselves.
In the beauty contest game where players choose a number between 0 and 100 and the winner picks closest to 2/3 of the average, the Nash equilibrium is 50.
Temporal discounting in behavioral economics is typically better described by exponential rather than hyperbolic functions.
The level-1 thinker in the beauty contest game best-responds to the assumption that all others choose randomly.
The Stroop effect demonstrates how automatic cognitive processes can interfere with deliberate ones.
In Spence's job market signaling model, education increases worker productivity and therefore serves as a valid quality signal.
The endowment effect is diminished when people trade in markets with high liquidity and frequent transactions.
Satisficing, as described by Herbert Simon, means choosing the first option that meets a minimum acceptability threshold.
The Asch conformity experiments showed that social pressure could lead people to give clearly incorrect answers.
In repeated games, the discount factor must be sufficiently low (near zero) for cooperation to be sustainable.
The Milgram obedience experiments demonstrated that ordinary people can administer apparently harmful shocks under authority.
The incentive compatibility constraint in mechanism design ensures that agents prefer to reveal their true type.
Choice overload suggests that more options always lead to better decision-making outcomes.
In the standard Rubinstein alternating-offers bargaining model with discounting, the first mover always receives the entire surplus.
The fundamental attribution error involves overweighting situational factors and underweighting dispositional ones when judging others.
Reciprocal altruism in evolutionary biology helps explain cooperative behavior between unrelated individuals.
The trolley problem is primarily used in game theory to study strategic interaction.
A correlated equilibrium can Pareto-dominate all Nash equilibria in a game.
The price of anarchy measures the efficiency loss from selfish behavior compared to a social optimum.
In Bayesian persuasion, the sender can never benefit from committing to a signal structure in advance.
Intertemporal choice involves tradeoffs between costs and benefits that occur at different points in time.
The competence-difficulty effect predicts that overconfidence decreases as task difficulty increases.
Giffen goods are inferior goods for which demand increases when price increases.
The gender gap in negotiation outcomes is consistently larger when the negotiating context is explicitly labeled as a negotiation.
In a Stackelberg competition, both firms simultaneously choose their quantities.
The hot hand belief is always a cognitive error with no basis in real-world sequential performance data.
The emotional cascade hypothesis in behavioral economics proposes that irrelevant emotional states can persistently influence sequential judgments.
In social dilemmas, group size has no effect on the likelihood of cooperative outcomes.
The notion of common knowledge is stronger than the notion of mutual knowledge.
The ostrich effect in behavioral finance refers to avoiding information about negative financial outcomes.
In cheap talk games, equilibrium communication is always fully separating.
The gender wage gap disappears entirely after controlling for hours worked and occupation.
Constructive memory refers to the brain accurately reproducing stored memories without distortion.
In global games, a unique equilibrium can be selected from games that otherwise have multiple equilibria.
The false consensus effect leads people to underestimate how many others share their beliefs.
Libertarian paternalism preserves freedom of choice while steering people toward beneficial defaults.
In competitive markets with externalities, the invisible hand leads to socially efficient outcomes.
The Matthew effect in sociology describes how initial advantages accumulate, giving the rich more and the poor less.
In the volunteer's dilemma, the unique Nash equilibrium involves exactly one player volunteering with certainty.
Dual process theory distinguishes between fast, intuitive (System 1) and slow, deliberate (System 2) thinking.
The certainty effect in prospect theory means people overweight outcomes that are certain relative to probable ones.
In a first-price sealed-bid auction with symmetric independent private values, rational bidders shade their bids below their true valuations.
The boomerang effect occurs when a persuasive message causes the audience to move further in the direction the message advocates.
Commitment devices help people overcome time-inconsistency by restricting future choices.
The revenue equivalence theorem states that under standard conditions, all common auction formats yield the same expected revenue to the seller.
In evolutionary game theory, a Nash equilibrium is always evolutionarily stable.
The zero-risk bias leads people to prefer the complete elimination of a small risk over a larger reduction in a larger risk.
Social norms are only enforced through formal legal sanctions.
The principle of insufficient reason assigns equal probability to all outcomes when no information is available.
The clustering illusion is the tendency to see patterns in random sequences.
In the Monty Hall problem, switching doors increases the probability of winning from 1/3 to 2/3.
The tragedy of the anticommons occurs when too many owners have exclusion rights, leading to underuse of a resource.
Inequity aversion means people prefer equal outcomes even when unequal outcomes give everyone more.
The disposition effect implies that investors' portfolios should exhibit a positive relationship between unrealized gains and time held.
Mechanism design is sometimes called reverse game theory because the designer sets the rules before agents play.
In a continuous double auction, prices consistently converge to the competitive equilibrium even with a small number of traders.
The isolation effect in prospect theory leads people to focus on the differences between options and ignore common components.
In Akerlof's market for lemons, high-quality sellers are driven out of the market by asymmetric information.
Behavioral game theory experimental results consistently confirm the theoretical prediction that players always choose Nash equilibrium strategies.
The ambiguity aversion documented in the Ellsberg paradox is fully explained by expected utility theory.
Self-serving bias leads individuals to attribute successes to their own ability and failures to external factors.
In a finitely repeated game, the last-period behavior is irrelevant to equilibrium in all preceding periods.
Social capital refers to the networks of relationships and trust that enable coordination and cooperation.
In Crawford and Sobel's model of strategic information transmission, full information revelation is always an equilibrium.
The reflection effect in prospect theory means people are risk-seeking over losses and risk-averse over gains.
The spiral of silence theory posits that people with minority opinions are less likely to express them publicly.
In the standard public goods experiment, contributions tend to start high and decrease over repeated rounds.
The concept of bounded rationality was introduced by Herbert Simon to describe decision-making with cognitive and informational limits.
In Rubinstein's alternating-offer bargaining, as the time between offers approaches zero, the first mover advantage disappears.
Norms of reciprocity tend to be weaker in large anonymous markets than in small communities.
In the stag hunt game, hunting stag is always the dominant strategy.
The focusing illusion describes how people overestimate the impact of any single factor on overall well-being.
In matching markets, the Gale-Shapley deferred acceptance algorithm always produces a stable matching.
Relative income hypothesis suggests people care more about their absolute income than how it compares to others.
The speed-accuracy tradeoff is a well-established phenomenon in cognitive psychology showing that faster decisions are more accurate.
Emotional contagion refers to the process by which people synchronize their emotions with those of others.
The Pigovian tax corrects a negative externality by taxing the activity at a rate equal to the marginal social damage.
In a sealed-bid common value auction, the Nash equilibrium bidding strategy requires bidders to ignore the winner's curse.
The hot-cold empathy gap describes the difficulty people have in predicting their own behavior in emotionally charged states.
In the centipede game, backward induction predicts that the first player immediately defects.
Framing effects are eliminated when decision-makers have high numeracy skills.
The tragedy of the commons was formally modeled and named by Garrett Hardin in 1968.
In a Bayesian Nash equilibrium, each player's strategy is a best response given beliefs about other players' types.
The cognitive miser metaphor captures the idea that people prefer effortful, systematic information processing.
Nudges in choice architecture always outperform financial incentives in changing behavior.
The superadditivity of value functions in combinatorial auctions means the value of a bundle can exceed the sum of its parts.
In social exchange theory, people terminate relationships when perceived costs exceed perceived benefits.
The planning paradox shows that adding more information about a project always reduces planning fallacy.
Preference reversals occur when choices between lotteries and valuations of the same lotteries are inconsistent.
In an all-pay auction, every bidder pays their bid regardless of whether they win.
The psychological immune system, as described by Daniel Gilbert, helps people rationalize and adapt to negative outcomes.
In evolutionary biology, kin selection can explain altruism through Hamilton's rule: rB > C.
The affect heuristic means that people assess the risks and benefits of activities based on their emotional feelings about them.
In the Fehr-Schmidt model, inequity aversion enters utility as a disutility from both advantageous and disadvantageous inequality.
Narrative economics, as proposed by Robert Shiller, argues that economic fluctuations are driven solely by rational expectations.
The mere measurement effect shows that asking people about their intentions increases the likelihood they perform the intended behavior.
In the hawk-dove game, the evolutionarily stable strategy always involves all individuals playing hawk.
The affect labeling effect shows that naming one's emotions reduces their intensity.
In network externalities, the value of a product to a user increases as more people use the same product.
Samaritan's dilemma describes how unconditional aid can reduce the recipient's incentive to be self-sufficient.
The optimal stopping problem (secretary problem) recommends rejecting all candidates until you have seen 1/e of the total pool.
The veil of ignorance in Rawls' framework leads to utilitarian rather than maximin principles of justice.
In the volunteer's dilemma, as the group size increases, the probability that at least one person volunteers decreases.
Cumulative prospect theory adds probability weighting to the original prospect theory to handle lotteries with more than two outcomes.
The Shapley value in cooperative game theory distributes payoffs based on each player's marginal contribution averaged over all possible orderings.
Attention economics treats attention as a scarce resource that can be allocated and competed for.
The cobra effect occurs when an incentive designed to solve a problem perversely makes it worse.
In strong Nash equilibrium, no coalition of players can jointly deviate and make all coalition members better off.
Construal level theory predicts that psychologically distant events are represented in more abstract terms.
The ultimatum game consistently shows that proposers are indifferent between offering 1 cent and offering 50% of the stake.
Functional fixedness is a cognitive bias where people can only perceive objects being used in their traditional way.
In global coordination games with strategic complementarities, equilibrium selection depends on the precision of private information.

The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.

Bats are blind.
Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
The capital of Australia is Sydney.
Spiders have eight legs.
The Sahara is the largest desert in the world.
Humans share about 98% of their DNA with chimpanzees.
Sound travels faster than light.
The moon has its own light.
A year on Mercury is shorter than a day on Mercury.
Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system.
The chemical symbol for gold is Au.
Penguins live in the Arctic.
The human brain uses about 20% of the body's energy.
All planets in our solar system rotate in the same direction.
Ostriches bury their heads in the sand.
The Great Barrier Reef is in Australia.
Diamonds are made of carbon.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
Sharks are mammals.
The Amazon Rainforest produces about 20% of the world's oxygen.
There are 7 continents on Earth.
The Titanic sank in 1912.
Napoleon Bonaparte was French by birth.
The human body has 4 lungs.
Light travels at approximately 300,000 km per second.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989.
